headline: Shares of semiconductor and chip companies are trading lower due to volatility initiated from South Korea after Samsung and SK Hynix fell, dragging down the KOPSI and U.S. peers. Also, weakness within the industry may result from growing energy costs after the U.S. and Iran exchanged blows and President Trump's recent comments.
